title: Indulgent Kenyan Potato Casserole
description: A rich and creamy Kenyan dish featuring clotted cream and potatoes, perfect for special occasions. This recipe is Jain-friendly and can be served at dinner parties.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, combine the sliced potatoes and enough cold water to cover them, then bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are slightly tender.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat, then add the chopped onions and cook until they are softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the curry powder, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked potatoes, onion mixture, and reduced red wine.
In a separate bowl, whip the clotted cream until it is smooth and creamy.
Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish with butter or oil, then add the potato mixture and top with the whipped clotted cream.
Bake for 30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is heated through.
